common.no_ussd_response: No response received from the operator.

multiple_modems.message: Multiple modems are configured. Specify config_entry_id or device_id.

no_test_sms_number.message: No test SMS number is configured.

invalid_phone_number.message: Invalid phone number. Use international format, for example +48501234567.

sms_index_not_found.message: SMS message with index {index} was not found.

notify_missing_number.message: Provide the recipient number in data.number.

user.title: GSM Modem
user.description: Connect your USB modem and choose the port where it responds. The port list is scanned automatically. SMS reply templates (5 fields) use Jinja2 — you can read Home Assistant entity states with states() and state_attr().

data_description.port: Choose a port from the list. Only ports where the modem responds are shown.
data_description.baudrate: Usually keep 115200. Change only if your modem documentation requires it.
data_description.scan_interval: How often the integration checks the modem and new SMS messages.
data_description.command_timeout: After this time the integration assumes the modem did not respond.
data_description.default_country_code: Country code without +, for example 48 for Poland or 39 for Italy. Used to convert local 9-digit numbers to +48XXXXXXXXX format.
data_description.sms_timeout: After this time the integration assumes the SMS send failed.
data_description.allowed_numbers: Numbers in +48XXXXXXXXX format (one per line or comma-separated). Only these numbers can send SMS commands. Example: +48501234567
data_description.delete_policy: Choose which received SMS messages are deleted automatically from the modem.
data_description.test_sms_number: Test number in +48XXXXXXXXX format, for example +48501234567. Used by the Send test SMS button. If empty, the first trusted number is used.
data_description.test_sms_message: Message sent by the test button.
data_description.balance_ussd_code: Operator code for checking balance, for example *101#.
data_description.enable_sms_commands: When enabled, messages from trusted numbers can trigger commands and automation events.
data_description.reply_to_sms_commands: When enabled, the modem sends an SMS reply after a recognized command (STATUS, BALANCE, HELP, or custom).
data_description.notify_unauthorized_sms: Show a Home Assistant notification when an SMS arrives from an unknown number.
data_description.purge_inbox_after_processing: When enabled (recommended), modem inbox is regularly cleaned to prevent full SMS memory and receive lockups.
data_description.status_command: Default: STATUS. The sender must text this exact word.
data_description.balance_command: Default: BALANCE. The integration will check balance using the code below.
data_description.help_command: Default: HELP. The modem replies with the available commands.
data_description.custom_commands: Comma-separated commands, for example GARAGE, GATE, ALARM. Each command fires its own automation event.
data_description.custom_command_reply_template: Default SMS text after a custom command (BRAMA, GATE, etc.) when no per-command line is set below. Jinja2 template: modem variables and Home Assistant states() are supported.
data_description.custom_command_reply_templates: Optional: separate template per command. One line each: COMMAND | Jinja2 body. Example: BRAMA | Gate: open. Lines starting with # are comments.
data_description.status_reply_template: Automatic SMS reply sent after the STATUS command. Jinja2 template. Modem variables: health, registration, operator, signal_percent, sim_state. You can add Home Assistant entities via states().
data_description.balance_reply_template: SMS reply sent after the BALANCE command. Jinja2 template. Variables: ussd_code, ussd_response.
data_description.help_reply_template: SMS reply sent after the HELP command. Jinja2 template. Variable commands contains the command list.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the modem on the selected port.
error.invalid_phone_numbers: Use international phone format with + and country code, for example +48501234567. Local 9-digit numbers also work when the country code above is set.

abort.already_configured: This modem has already been added.

error.invalid_phone_numbers: Use international phone format with + and country code, for example +48501234567. Local 9-digit numbers also work when the country code above is set.

init.title: GSM Modem settings
init.description: Change refresh, SMS commands and inbox behavior without editing YAML. There are 5 template fields: STATUS, BALANCE, HELP, default custom-command template, and optional per-command lines (COMMAND | template).

data_description.scan_interval: How often the integration checks the modem and new SMS messages.
data_description.command_timeout: After this time the integration assumes the modem did not respond.
data_description.default_country_code: Country code without +, for example 48 for Poland or 39 for Italy. Used to convert local 9-digit numbers to +48XXXXXXXXX format.
data_description.sms_timeout: After this time the integration assumes the SMS send failed.
data_description.allowed_numbers: Numbers in +48XXXXXXXXX format (one per line or comma-separated). Example: +48501234567
data_description.delete_policy: Choose which received SMS messages are deleted automatically from the modem.
data_description.test_sms_number: Test number in +48XXXXXXXXX format, for example +48501234567. Used by the Send test SMS button.
data_description.test_sms_message: Message sent by the test button.
data_description.balance_ussd_code: Operator code for checking balance, for example *101#.
data_description.enable_sms_commands: When enabled, messages from trusted numbers can trigger commands and automation events.
data_description.reply_to_sms_commands: When enabled, the modem sends an SMS reply after a recognized command (STATUS, BALANCE, HELP, or custom).
data_description.notify_unauthorized_sms: Show a notification when an SMS arrives from an unknown number.
data_description.purge_inbox_after_processing: When enabled (recommended), modem inbox is regularly cleaned to prevent full SMS memory and receive lockups.
data_description.status_command: Word sent by SMS to request modem status.
data_description.balance_command: Word sent by SMS to check balance.
data_description.help_command: Word sent by SMS to receive the command list.
data_description.custom_commands: Comma-separated commands, for example GARAGE, ALARM. Each command fires its own automation event.
data_description.custom_command_reply_template: Default SMS text after a custom command (BRAMA, GATE, etc.) when no per-command line is set below. Jinja2 template: modem variables and Home Assistant states() are supported.
data_description.custom_command_reply_templates: Optional: separate template per command. One line each: COMMAND | Jinja2 body. Example: BRAMA | Gate: open. Lines starting with # are comments.
data_description.status_reply_template: Automatic SMS reply sent after the STATUS command. Jinja2 template. Modem variables: health, registration, operator, signal_percent, sim_state. You can add Home Assistant entities via states().
data_description.balance_reply_template: SMS reply sent after the BALANCE command. Jinja2 template. Variables: ussd_code, ussd_response.
data_description.help_reply_template: SMS reply sent after the HELP command. Jinja2 template. Variable commands contains the command list.
